rationale: Both catch the real latent gap (unswept get_cache_scale caller in the diffusion transformer), satisfying gap_hit for each. X pads its findings with two speculative 'dead code' nitpicks and a trivial missing-comment nit, and its evidence trail is internally contradictory (claims a repo-wide grep found both '1 survivor' and '0 hits' for the same pattern), undercutting precision; its REQUEST CHANGES verdict also overstates severity relative to what actually happened (PR was approved/merged). Y's review is more tightly grounded — it traces the actual AutoWeightsLoader call chain with real upstream line citations to justify why the delegated-loader removals are safe (closely matching the human reviewer's own reasoning), surfaces a sharper test-coverage gap (delegated-loader test bypasses the real mapping it claims to verify), and frames the diffusion-transformer gap with accurate historical context (later fixed) rather than treating it as an urgent blocker, better matching ground truth and reality.
